description_long: This object is a cylindrical, stick-handled bomb resembling the classic German Stielhandgranate or 'stick grenade.' The main body consists of a dark, metallic cylindrical head attached to a long, wooden handle, which is slightly tapered and features a metal cap at the end. The metal head appears robust and slightly weathered, with a matte finish and subtle surface irregularities. The handle is made of wood, showing a natural grain and a smooth, polished surface, with a metal ring or collar securing the head to the shaft. The overall construction suggests a utilitarian design, with the handle providing leverage for throwing. No visible wires, buttons, or digital components are present, and the object lacks any clear warning labels or timer displays.
description: A stick-handled bomb with a dark metal cylindrical head and a long, polished wooden handle, resembling a classic stick grenade.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the bomb, highlighting the cylindrical metal head and the wooden handle with a metal cap.
description_view_1: Front view looking directly at the metal head, with the handle receding behind and the circular face of the head prominent.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the cylindrical head and handle from the other side.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the end of the wooden handle, with the metal cap visible and the head mostly obscured.
attribute_detonation_mechanism: The detonation mechanism is likely initiated by unscrewing or striking the end cap, which may activate an internal fuse or percussion system.
